what_it_does: This file tells Git which files and directories to ignore in the company/recollection repository, including dependencies, lockfiles, temporary files, logs, OS files, SQLite databases, environment secrets, local Claude settings, and a generated version source file.
observations: Includes a comment noting src/version.ts is generated by scripts/generate-version.js
standouts: Generated source file src/version.ts is ignored with a generator script comment
SQLite database artifacts are excluded broadly
conventions: Follows common Node.js .gitignore conventions (node_modules, logs, .env, OS files)
Uses a comment to document the generator for an ignored file
Ignores package-lock.json, which is sometimes committed in Node projects
concerns: Ignoring package-lock.json may reduce dependency reproducibility
Generated src/version.ts under src/ implies build-time generation is required before compilation
No distinction between production and development .env files
notes: A standard repository ignore list with a notable generated-source exception and local AI-tool settings exclusion.
questions: Is package-lock.json intentionally ignored across all environments?
Are the *.db files local development databases, test fixtures, or production artifacts?
What does .claude/settings.local.json contain and why must it be ignored?
Is src/version.ts regenerated on every build or only at release?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose (ignore listed files) matches what it does.
intention_signals: It appears designed to keep machine-specific, generated, and sensitive files out of version control while documenting the source of the generated version file.
novelty: The explicit ignore for .claude/settings.local.json and the documented generated src/version.ts entry are relatively distinctive.
contribution: It contributes a set of ignore rules that keep dependency directories, build artifacts, logs, OS files, local databases, secrets, local AI settings, and generated source out of version control.
summary_for_embedding: The .gitignore file for company/recollection excludes Node dependencies, lockfiles, temporary and log files, OS files, SQLite databases, environment secrets, local Claude settings, and the generated src/version.ts file.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to prevent machine-local, generated, and sensitive files from being committed to the repository.
